name: System Sans-Serif
css: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, "Noto Sans", "Liberation Sans", sans-serif, "Apple Color Emoji", "Segoe UI Emoji", "Segoe UI Symbol", "Noto Color Emoji"
description: The standard native sans-serif stack used by Bootstrap 5 and GitHub. Resolves to the platform's default UI font: San Francisco on macOS/iOS, Segoe UI on Windows, and Roboto on Android.
usage: Body text, UI elements, general-purpose web typography.

name: System Serif
css: Georgia, Cambria, "Times New Roman", Times, serif
description: A safe cross-platform serif stack that prefers Georgia for its on-screen legibility. Falls back to Cambria on Windows and Times New Roman for maximum compatibility.
usage: Editorial content, long-form reading, formal documents.

name: System Monospace
css: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", "Courier New", monospace
description: The system monospace stack used by GitHub for code blocks. Resolves to SF Mono on Apple platforms, Consolas on Windows, and Liberation Mono on Linux.
usage: Code blocks, inline code, terminal output, technical documentation.

name: System UI
description: A minimal stack using the CSS 'system-ui' keyword, which maps directly to the operating system's default UI font. Provides the most native feel with the fewest overrides.
usage: UI components, dashboards, native-feeling web applications.

name: Geometric Sans-Serif
css: Inter, "Nunito Sans", "DM Sans", Poppins, Outfit, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif
description: A modern geometric sans-serif stack led by Inter — designed specifically for screen legibility. Clean, neutral, and widely used in SaaS and developer products.
usage: Product UIs, dashboards, landing pages, modern web applications.

name: Humanist Sans-Serif
css: "Gill Sans", "Gill Sans MT", Calibri, "Trebuchet MS", "Fira Sans", Optima, "Myriad Pro", sans-serif
description: A humanist sans-serif stack with warmer, more organic letterforms than geometric alternatives. Combines readability with a friendly, approachable character.
usage: Marketing copy, editorial layouts, brand-facing content, signage.

name: Transitional Serif
css: Georgia, "Book Antiqua", Palatino, "Palatino Linotype", "Bitstream Charter", Charter, serif
description: A transitional serif stack centered on Georgia, which bridges old-style and modern serif design. Excellent screen legibility at body sizes due to its open apertures and generous x-height.
usage: Long-form articles, blogs, news sites, academic content.

name: Old-Style Serif
css: Palatino, "Palatino Linotype", "Palatino LT STD", "Book Antiqua", Garamond, "EB Garamond", "Hoefler Text", serif
description: An old-style (humanist) serif stack with diagonal stress, low stroke contrast, and bracketed serifs characteristic of 15th–17th century type design. Evokes a classic, literary quality.
usage: Literary publications, academic writing, formal correspondence, historical content.

name: Slab Serif
css: Rockwell, "Rockwell Extra Bold", "Courier New", "Roboto Slab", "Zilla Slab", "Crete Round", serif
description: A slab serif (Egyptian) stack with thick, block-like serifs and low stroke contrast. Strong and assertive, combining the legibility of sans-serif with the character of serif design.
usage: Headlines, poster text, branding, editorial headers, tech brand identities.

name: Modern Serif (Didone)
css: Didot, "Didot LT STD", "Bodoni MT", "Bodoni 72", "GFS Didot", "Libre Bodoni", "Playfair Display", serif
description: A Didone (modern) serif stack with extreme contrast between thick and hairline strokes, vertical stress, and unbracketed serifs. Highly refined and elegant at display sizes.
usage: Fashion editorial, luxury branding, high-contrast headlines, magazine layouts.

name: Code Monospace
css: "JetBrains Mono", "Fira Code", "Cascadia Code", "Source Code Pro", Hack, Inconsolata, "Courier New", monospace
description: A developer-focused monospace stack leading with modern coding fonts that support ligatures and have been specifically designed for long coding sessions. JetBrains Mono and Fira Code are the most widely installed.
usage: Code editors, syntax-highlighted code blocks, REPLs, developer documentation.

name: Terminal Monospace
css: "Courier New", Courier, "Lucida Console", Monaco, "Andale Mono", monospace
description: A classic terminal-style monospace stack centered on Courier New. Evokes the aesthetic of typewriters, command-line interfaces, and early computing.
usage: Terminal emulators, typewriter-style effects, retro/hacker aesthetics, receipts.

name: Rounded Sans-Serif
css: Nunito, "Varela Round", "M PLUS Rounded 1c", Quicksand, Comfortaa, "Arial Rounded MT Bold", sans-serif
description: A soft, rounded sans-serif stack with curved terminals that conveys friendliness and approachability. Widely used in children's products, wellness apps, and casual consumer-facing interfaces.
usage: Children's content, wellness apps, friendly consumer UIs, casual brands, onboarding flows.

name: Display Sans-Serif
css: "Bebas Neue", Anton, "Barlow Condensed", Oswald, Impact, "Arial Narrow", sans-serif
description: A condensed display sans-serif stack designed for maximum visual impact at large sizes. Impact is a universally available fallback for when web fonts are unavailable.
usage: Hero headings, posters, advertising banners, sports and entertainment branding.

name: Handwriting
css: "Dancing Script", Pacifico, Caveat, "Patrick Hand", "Indie Flower", "Bradley Hand", "Comic Sans MS", cursive
description: A handwriting-style stack that mimics natural script letterforms. Modern web fonts like Dancing Script and Caveat are far more refined than the native cursive fallback.
usage: Wedding and event invitations, personal blogs, greeting cards, decorative accents, craft and food brands.
